title: santolina chamaecyparissus var. intricata
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus var. intricata, also known as Gray Santolina or Intricate Wormwood, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and small yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Santolina chamaecyparissus var. intricata have been traditionally used to soothe digestive problems, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, offering a range of health benefits. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
